source:
His objection had three parts: (a) that the evidence was drawn entirely from one country; (b) that the effect vanished when the wealthiest households were excluded; and (c) that the author had not reported how many models were tried before this one was published. Only the third was answered, and it was answered badly, in a reply that conceded the practice while denying that it mattered here.

The first two objections are still, forty years on, the standard reasons the finding is taught as a caution rather than a result.

expect.why: A lettered list label needs a spoken ordinal.
The second label in the same enumeration.
The third label in the same enumeration.

prepared:
His objection had three parts: first, that the evidence was drawn entirely from one country; second, that the effect vanished when the wealthiest households were excluded; and third, that the author had not reported how many models were tried before this one was published. Only the third was answered, and it was answered badly, in a reply that conceded the practice while denying that it mattered here.

The first two objections are still, forty years on, the standard reasons the finding is taught as a caution rather than a result.
notes: An inline lettered enumeration where each label needs its own small edit into a spoken ordinal. A model that deletes the labels leaves three clauses running together, and one that rewrites the whole sentence into a list is refused as a rewrite.
